findings.evidence: `TauCeti/.../RamificationInertia.lean:38` calls `ncard_primesOver_mul_ramificationIdxIn_mul_inertiaDegIn P B G`; Mathlib declares that theorem with `(p : Ideal A) [p.IsPrime]` at `Mathlib/NumberTheory/RamificationInertia/Galois.lean:216-222`.
findings.fix: Change the assumption to `[P.IsPrime]` and update the docstring from “nonzero maximal ideal” to “prime ideal”; the private wrapper in `SplitsCompletely.lean` can make the same relaxation or follow from the generalized theorem.
findings.issue: `ncard_primesOver_eq_natCard_iff_of_isGaloisGroup` requires `[P.IsMaximal]`, but its proof only uses Mathlib’s prime-ideal fundamental identity, so the public API is under-generalized.

summary: The updated wrapper still states the criterion at a stronger-than-needed level: Mathlib’s refactored fundamental identity now works for prime ideals, not only maximal ideals.
